expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 heading reads 'Enforcement: TDD red-green-refactor loop (required on every track)' and explicitly enumerates Red (failing tests), Green (minimum code to pass), and Refactor (clean up without breaking tests) steps.

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier is launched as a subagent for refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: Section 6.1 states 'An independent code-simplifier subagent is launched after implementation' and describes its role scanning for 'duplication, unnecessary complexity, and clarity issues.'

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: Section 6.2 explicitly names 'multiple independent code-reviewer agents run in parallel' with three distinct focus areas (simplicity, correctness, conventions), each reading final code independently.

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Response selects Standard track with two code-explorer agents, limits architecture options to 'up to 2' when a real choice exists, and explicitly notes 'the skill explicitly avoids manufacturing options when the choice isn't meaningful'—no heavy-track escalation.
